LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER REPRESENTED IN OPEN COURT THAT HE NOTIFIED THE OTHER PARTY REGARDING HIS APPEARANCE TO CONTEST THE RULING. HENCE, THE COURT ALLOWED HIM TO ARGUE THE MATTER. THEREAFTER, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAIN WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. Off Calendar Dec-17-2007 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substantial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, it is hereby ordered that this matter is dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Proceedings reported by: Helen Sorep-Motley, CSR# 9225. (212)
